How_implemented: Each agent is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter: name, description (auto-routing trigger), model (e.g. sonnet), maxTurns (e.g. 20), and a least-privilege tools allowlist (seo-technical: Read, Bash, Write, Glob, Grep). Body is the system prompt encoding the specialist's analysis framework (seo-technical: 9 categories, 2026 CWV thresholds, INP-not-FID rule, 0-100 score). Persona = named SEO discipline. Model-per-agent and tool-scoping are set in frontmatter.

The audit agent crawls the homepage, detects business type, then spawns up to 15 subagents — 8 always-active (seo-technical, seo-content, seo-schema, seo-sitemap, seo-performance, seo-visual, seo-geo, seo-sxo) in parallel via Task — and synthesizes their findings into a 7-category weighted health score and prioritized ACTION-PLAN.md. Planner/dispatcher producing a delegation plan, then fanning out workers concurrently.

7 conditional specialists spawn only when gating signals are met: seo-local on Local-Service industry detection; seo-maps when Local AND DataForSEO MCP present; seo-ecommerce on E-commerce detection; seo-cluster on content-strategy signals (blog/pillar/topic-cluster); seo-drift when a baseline exists for the URL; seo-google/seo-backlinks when credential checks pass. A dependency/availability-gated fleet rather than blind fan-out — checker-gated parallelism keyed on industry + credentials + prior state.

Pattern_raw_name: Graceful-degradation delegation ('if available, otherwise run inline sequentially')
How_implemented: The audit skill's delegation instruction is 'Delegate to subagents (if available, otherwise run inline sequentially)' — the same workflow runs whether or not the harness supports subagents, falling back to a sequential single-context execution. Portability-aware orchestration.

Pattern_raw_name: 5 parallel marketing-dimension subagents (market-content, market-conversion, market-competitive, market-technical, market-strategy)
How_implemented: agents/<name>.md are plain-Markdown system prompts (NO frontmatter, no tools/model field — earlier auto-summaries that claimed `tools:`/`model:` were hallucinated; the raw files open directly with '# ... Subagent' then 'You are a ... specialist'). The market-audit orchestrator launches all 5 simultaneously ('Launch all 5 subagents simultaneously using Claude Code's subagent capability'), each handed the same business-type + page-map + fetched content. Each owns exactly one scoring dimension, fetches its own pages via WebFetch, scores 5 sub-dimensions 0-10 against explicit per-band rubrics (e.g. Headline Clarity 9-10 vs 7-8 vs 5-6...), and RETURNS a fixed-structure block (score table + Top Wins + Critical Fixes + Before/After rewrites + Missing Elements). The orchestrator consumes only those structured return blocks, not each agent's raw page-fetch transcript.

Pattern_raw_name: cs-* persona agents (93 total; e.g. cs-handoff-author, CFO/CRO/CISO advisors)
How_implemented: Agents are markdown files with YAML frontmatter: name, a rich trigger-laden description (explicit + implicit invocation signals), tools allowlist (e.g. cs-handoff-author: [Read,Write,Edit,Bash,Grep,Glob] — least-privilege), and model: inherit. Body = a tight persona system prompt with a mandated workflow (cs-handoff-author: verify handoff_prompt.md checklist → run redaction linter strict mode → load target dir via config_loader.py → write 5-section doc, cap skills 3-5). C-level domain ships 13+ distinct voiced personas (CFO≠CRO≠CISO enforced by 'voice differentiation').

Pattern_raw_name: Orchestrator personas + 'no silent chaining' router rule
How_implemented: ORCHESTRATION.md Pattern A (Solo Sprint: sequential persona switching by phase) and Pattern B (Domain Deep-Dive: one persona stacking multiple skills). Root CLAUDE.md hard rule: orchestrators MUST ask permission before routing to sub-skills; deterministic routers score confidence thresholds explicitly and, below threshold, ask one question with a recommended answer — a gated dispatcher→specialist mechanism rather than silent fan-out.

Sequential cross-disciplinary review: startup-cto designs architecture → growth-marketer evaluates from market angle → solo-founder arbitrates trade-offs, with a structured handoff summary (decisions/artifacts/open questions) carried between personas. A reviewer/critic chain that also transfers working knowledge to the next persona (fresh-context handoff), though personas share one session.

Two specialist agents inside the self-improving plugin: memory-analyst (invoked by /si:review) reads MEMORY.md, finds recurring promotion candidates and consolidation groups; skill-extractor packages proven patterns into standalone skills. Each is a markdown+frontmatter agent with a scoped job in the curation pipeline.

Pattern_raw_name: Pre-configured persona loadouts (Startup CTO / Growth Marketer / Solo Founder)
How_implemented: Three bundled personas ship with curated skill loadouts that get activated as a panel for a phase of work (e.g. a 6-week launch cycles personas through build→marketing→shipping). Team-preset style invocation rather than individually wired agents.

Pattern_raw_name: Memory Flush sub-agent (memory_flush.py via Agent SDK)
How_implemented: A background Claude Agent SDK process spawned by the PreCompact/SessionEnd hooks, run with allowed_tools=[] (zero-tool, least-privilege) — its only job is to read the dumped conversation temp file and reason about what to persist, appending a distilled summary to the daily log. Pure reasoning agent acting as a context-handoff/memory-transfer worker from the dying session.

Pattern_raw_name: Heartbeat reasoning agent + pre-flight guardrail agent
How_implemented: The heartbeat's Claude phase is a scheduled Agent SDK invocation that reasons over Python-preloaded integration data and decides what merits a notification. The PRD's Phase 6/8 add a 'pre-flight guardrail agent' that vets proposed actions before execution. These are prescribed by the PRD/blueprint rather than shipped as standalone .md agent definitions in this repo.

Workflow commands fan out specialized parallel subagents by domain — save spawns people/projects/tasks/decisions/ideas/content agents that each search-then-write their slice; ingest spawns entities/concepts/projects/contradictions agents that each rewrite their page class concurrently. The main command acts as dispatcher/aggregator, then reports a consolidated 'what was saved + where' list (compact summary returns, not raw agent output).

/obsidian-reconcile fans out claims/entity/decisions/source-freshness agents; /obsidian-synthesize fans out cross-source/entity-convergence/concept-evolution/orphan-rescue agents. Each works a different contradiction or pattern dimension concurrently; results merge into rewrites + synthesis pages with logged counts. The source-freshness/contradiction agents act as adversarial verifiers against the existing vault state.

Decision deliberation across personas — custom advisor notes from an Advisors/ folder (each persona note used as a distinct lens) or a generic fallback panel (skeptic, user/customer, operator, long-game). Step 3 each persona gives an INDEPENDENT verdict (position, strongest reasoning, conditions to change) with explicit 'keep them genuinely distinct - do not let them converge prematurely'; Step 4 synthesis surfaces the split rather than forcing consensus ('the split is the most useful output'). Single-agent role-play, not separate spawned processes.

Background agents run via obsidian-bg-agent.sh + obsidian-bg-agent.hook.yaml on a schedule: Morning 8AM (daily note + overdue tasks), Nightly 10PM (close day, reconcile contradictions, synthesize patterns, heal orphans), Weekly Fri 6PM (weekly review), Health Sun 9PM (vault audit). 'Maintain the vault while you sleep' — each fires the corresponding command pipeline unattended.

Pattern_raw_name: Minions — Postgres-native durable subagent queue (one queue for shell + LLM jobs)
How_implemented: skills/minion-orchestrator/SKILL.md exposes a single durable/observable/steerable queue backed by Postgres: jobs survive gateway restart, every job carries structured progress (step/total, last tool), token accounting (input/output/cache_read rolling up child->parent), and a full session transcript. Lifecycle ops (pause/resume/cancel/replay, replay with data_overrides) are MCP-callable; mid-flight STEERING via send_job_message injects a directive read on each agent iteration. Two lanes: deterministic shell jobs (CLI-only, gated by GBRAIN_ALLOW_SHELL_JOBS=1) and LLM subagent jobs (gbrain agent run, --tools restricted to a BRAIN_TOOL_ALLOWLIST, --model/--max-turns/--timeout-ms per job). PROTECTED_JOB_NAMES blocks MCP submission of shell/subagent jobs (agent observes, operator submits).

Pattern_raw_name: Fan-out manifest: N child subagents + 1 aggregator that claims after all children terminate
How_implemented: `gbrain agent run --fanout-manifest companies.json` submits a manifest describing N children (each handler name='subagent') + 1 aggregator (name='subagent_aggregator') that only claims AFTER every child terminates. Parent-child DAGs with configurable failure policies; child tokens roll up to parent on completion. The dream cycle uses the same pattern: one Sonnet subagent fanned out per worth-processing transcript, orchestrator collects new slugs from subagent_tool_executions (not pages.updated_at) to avoid picking up unrelated writes.

Pattern_raw_name: Pain-triggered routing: native subagent first, durable Minion on specific pain signals
How_implemented: skills/conventions/subagent-routing.md reads ~/.gbrain/preferences.json minion_mode (always | pain_triggered[default] | off) before every spawn. In pain_triggered mode the agent defaults to zero-setup native subagents and only OFFERS the durable-Minion switch when a concrete pain signal fires: gateway restart mid-task, state-drop ('why did you forget'), parallel >3, runtime >5min, or explicit user frustration. An inline vs background decision table (single tool <30s / read-only / user-waiting -> inline; multi-step / parallel 2+ / survive-restart / wants-progress -> Minion). Concurrency budget: stagger when active jobs >5.

Pattern_raw_name: Context-firewall convention propagated through any subagent depth
How_implemented: brain-first.md notes sub-agents inherit gbrain tools but not the knowledge of when to use them, and mandates that when an agent spawns further sub-agents it MUST inject 'Read skills/conventions/brain-first.md before starting work' into the child's task prompt — so the convention propagates through any depth of chain. Subagent jobs run with a least-privilege --tools allow-list (rejected at submit if outside BRAIN_TOOL_ALLOWLIST); the dream synthesizer additionally runs under an allowed_slug_prefixes write allow-list.

Pattern_raw_name: Role-as-skill virtual software team (23+ slash commands = CEO/eng-mgr/designer/QA/security/release-eng personas)
How_implemented: The whole product is a roster of named-role slash commands forming a virtual team along a fixed sprint: /office-hours (founder/PM brainstorm), /plan-ceo-review (CEO scope), /plan-eng-review (architect), /plan-design-review (designer), /plan-devex-review (DX), /review (staff engineer audit), /qa (QA), /cso (security/OWASP+STRIDE), /ship & /land-and-deploy (release engineer), /investigate (debugger), /learn (knowledge manager). Each role is a skill, not a separate agent process — the persona is encoded in the SKILL.md body + tier preamble (voice directive). The roles chain Think->Plan->Build->Review->Test->Ship->Reflect.

Pattern_raw_name: review-army — parallel fan-out specialist reviewers with consensus dedup
How_implemented: scripts/resolvers/review-army.ts conditionally selects up to 7 specialist reviewers (Testing+Maintainability always-on for >50-line diffs; Security[auth/backend], Performance, Data-Migration, API-Contract, Design conditional on detected scope) plus a Red-Team reviewer (if diff>200 lines or any CRITICAL exists). Explicit instruction: 'Launch ALL selected specialists in a single message (multiple Agent tool calls) so they run in parallel', each with FRESH context (no prior-review bias). Findings deduped by {path}:{line}:{category} fingerprint; multi-specialist agreement bumps confidence +1 (cap 10) and tags 'MULTI-SPECIALIST CONFIRMED'; confidence 1-4 suppressed.

Pattern_raw_name: Dual-voice cross-model review (Claude subagent + Codex) with consensus table
How_implemented: Within /autoplan each review phase runs TWO independent voices sequentially in foreground: a Claude subagent via the Agent tool that reads the plan with NO prior context (fresh-context verifier, avoids confirmation bias), and Codex (OpenAI, Bash exec, 10-min timeout) which receives prior-phase consensus summaries for cross-phase awareness. Outputs build a consensus table (CONFIRMED=both agree, DISAGREE=taste decision surfaced at the gate). /codex and /review (Claude) overlap-detection is the same idea at top level — consensus recommendations when both models agree.

Pattern_raw_name: Independent spec-review subagent (quality-gate loop, max 3 iterations)
How_implemented: office-hours' handoff section dispatches an independent subagent reviewer that audits the design doc on 5 dimensions (completeness/consistency/clarity/scope/feasibility), returns a 1-10 score + PASS or numbered issues; issues are fixed and the reviewer is RE-DISPATCHED up to 3 times; unresolved items get logged as 'Reviewer Concerns' for downstream skills to read. Metrics appended to ~/.gstack/analytics/spec-review.jsonl.

Pattern_raw_name: Conductor parallel sprints + Codex CLI adapter
How_implemented: conductor.json wires Conductor integration to run 10-15 parallel sprints in ISOLATED workspaces, each with independent /office-hours, /review, /qa, /ship execution (separate API keys GSTACK_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GSTACK_OPENAI_API_KEY for paid evals). /pair-agent coordinates Claude Code with OpenClaw/Hermes/Codex in a shared browser with scoped tokens and tab isolation. agents/openai.yaml is the Codex host adapter.

~33+ agent definitions as markdown+frontmatter: name, description (states what spawns it, e.g. 'Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator'), tools (least-privilege allowlist per agent — researcher gets WebSearch/Exa/Firecrawl/context7, executor gets Edit/Bash but no web), and color. Distinct role personas across the lifecycle: researchers (phase/domain/project/ai/advisor/ui), planner, plan-checker, executor, verifier, code-reviewer, security-auditor, ui-auditor, doc-synthesizer/classifier/verifier, codebase-mapper, pattern-mapper, nyquist-auditor, etc.

plan-phase orchestrator runs three agents serially: gsd-phase-researcher (→RESEARCH.md, conditional on flags) → gsd-planner (→PLAN.md) → gsd-plan-checker (→VERIFICATION.md). On checker failure it re-spawns the planner with the checker's structured YAML feedback in a revision loop, max 3 iterations before escalation. Each agent is a fresh context receiving prior artifacts as the handoff baton.

execute-phase orchestrator discovers all plans in a phase, computes a dependency graph, groups plans into parallel-safe waves, and dispatches each wave's plans to independent gsd-executor subagents via the Agent tool — each spawned with a fresh ~100% context budget plus its plan. Later waves gate on earlier-wave completion (dependency-wave fleet). Phase verification by gsd-verifier runs only after all waves complete.

Fresh-context skeptic that starts from 'falsify first' — hypothesis: tasks completed but goal missed; explicitly distrusts SUMMARY.md and treats file existence as insufficient. Applies a 4-level artifact hierarchy: Exists → Substantive (real code not stub) → Wired (imported/used) → Data Flows (dynamic, not hardcoded/empty); a component rendering an empty array is HOLLOW not verified. Scans for debt markers (TBD/FIXME/XXX), runs behavioral probes with pass/fail exit codes, and emits YAML gaps frontmatter consumable by /gsd:plan-phase --gaps.

Parallel executors run in git worktrees (per-agent branches); executor refuses to commit on protected refs, asserts its per-agent branch, and is forbidden from `git clean` in worktrees (would destroy concurrent agents' work). CONTEXT.md carries WORKTREE.SEAM predicates and worktree-safety tests guard the invariant.

TypeScript SDK programmatically drives the multi-stage agent workflow: init-runner / phase-runner / session-runner / milestone-runner stage execution, prompt-builder + prompt-sanitizer compose and clean each agent's prompt, and query-* adapters (native + subprocess) invoke the underlying runtime — config-as-code orchestration sitting beneath the markdown commands.

Each agent = markdown + frontmatter (name, description, model:sonnet). Set: worker-researcher (WebSearch/WebFetch, no synthesis), worker-data-collector, worker-file-ops, worker-publisher, worker-executor (pre-approved mutations only), brief-people-updater. Model-tiered by design: workers run Sonnet for cost, the lead runs Opus for reasoning (CLAUDE.md Model Routing table). Tool-scoped: each agent lists only the capabilities it needs (e.g. researcher = search/fetch/reads; executor = Jira/Linear/GitHub/curl).

CLAUDE.md hard rule: 'If it doesn't need judgment, delegate. Lead handles thinking only.' Lead decides + synthesizes; workers do data collection / I/O / publishing / pre-approved mutations and return status+path. worker-executor explicitly: 'You do NOT decide what to do — the lead already decided' — pure execution, no improvisation, continues on partial failure and reports which actions succeeded/failed.

agent_mode:team launches N specialist subagents in ONE message: team-brief fans out 6 named collectors (github/slack/meeting/linear/braindump/posthog) concurrently; auto-research launches 5-7 worker-researcher threads with run_in_background:true. Orchestrator then performs cross-reference synthesis. agent_mode:solo collapses to sequential passes in the main conversation. Mode read per-skill from MY-PROFILE.md.

Not personas that execute, but onboarding-time config presets: Product Manager / Engineering Lead / Engineer / Designer / Founder / Marketer (+custom). Each maps the role to recommended skills + essential integrations and a default agent_mode (PM pack -> 10 skills, integrations GitHub/Linear/Slack/PostHog/Notion, team mode). Skills carry matching roles:[...] frontmatter so recommendations are bidirectional; onboarding skill matches the user to a pack and writes MY-PROFILE.md accordingly.

Agent-definition markdown (frontmatter name/description/tools/model). One researcher spawned per query per round, all in parallel; each searches Obsidian/Readwise/NLM/YouTube seeds, weights user-created content (NLM notes, Readwise highlights) above raw imports, caps at ~15 findings, dedups, returns findings JSON (title, original_path, origin, relevance, topic-specific summary, key_concepts, author/date/url). Tool scope limited to search CLIs + read.

Runs BETWEEN rounds; reads only findings metadata (jq), never source files. Maps discoveries to original key themes, grades each well-covered (3+)/thin(1-2)/missing(0), generates exactly N next-round queries targeting underrepresented themes while avoiding prior search strings; later rounds narrow toward specific tools/frameworks/authors. Returns a queries-manifest JSON so the orchestrator configures the next cycle without re-reading sources — a planner that steers worker waves.

One per raw source, in parallel. The sole raw-file reader (context firewall, see CM-06). Tools strictly Read + Write (single md file). Writes wiki/sources/<slug>.md (Summary/Key claims/Notable quotes/Distinctive framing/Connections) with anchored wikilink citations; returns referenced + suggested-new entity/concept slugs JSON. No side effects on index or other pages.

One per qualifying entity/concept slug, in parallel. Reads ONLY in-scope source pages (never raw); aggregates definition/claims/quotes/relationships/tensions/open-questions across sources; computes source_count/mention_count/confidence; word/quote caps (~300-700 words, <=125 char quotes); preserves <!-- KEEP --> edits; byte-identical noop skip; returns created/updated/noop JSON. Page created only when mention_count>=2 across distinct sources.

Two spawned in parallel — one writes overview.md (topic catalog + source breakdown), one writes synthesis.md (thesis integrating findings). Both discover wiki state via bash; never preloaded into orchestrator context (keeps orchestrator lean).

Fetches/copies raw source content into raw/ (handling fetch/curl/transcript/PDF extraction) so source_writers have local files; explicitly no longer writes index.yaml (responsibility moved to a deterministic script). A pipeline-stage worker.
Writes ARCHITECTURE.md + per-module docs for an ingested GitHub repo; ARCHITECTURE acts as the module index with inline relative wikilinks.

Writes A-vs-B comparison pages (alphabetized pair slug, e.g. bm25-vs-hybrid-retrieval.md) from entity/concept source pages when an entity pair has >=2 mutual links — synthesizing a dedicated comparison artifact.

Pattern_raw_name: Three-agent roster: opencode / Hermes / Gemini CLI
How_implemented: Three named role-personas with distinct model/provider tiers: opencode (code/DevOps, deepseek-v4-flash-free via opencode-zen), Hermes (memory+scheduling+notifications, Owl Alpha on OpenRouter), Gemini CLI (research/analysis, Google OAuth). Each lives in agents/<name>/ with its own config. Roles are scoped by capability and all pinned to free/cheap models — model-tiering by task type.

AGENTS.md routing rules classify each task: code/infra -> opencode, memory/scheduling -> hermes, research/analysis -> gemini, ambiguous -> opencode fallback. A keyword-match-with-confidence-score router suggests the agent; routing config persists in data/agent-routes.json. Dispatcher -> specialist pattern.

Multi-step requests chain agents in a fixed pipeline: Gemini researches -> opencode implements -> Hermes monitors, with each step's brain/skill artifacts (specs, learnings, audit) read by the next. Document-handoff is the baton between runtimes.

Pattern_raw_name: Resilient failover via primary/fallback agent assignment
How_implemented: Constitution principle 5 (never block on a single point of failure) plus every skill's primary+fallback declaration means the router degrades gracefully to the fallback runtime when the primary agent is unavailable — failover is encoded at skill granularity, not just globally.

Canonical Queen+workers hive-mind. queen-coordinator.md (frontmatter: name + description only) is 'sovereign orchestrator' with hierarchical/democratic/emergency governance modes; allocates resources (30% collective-intelligence, 40% workers, 20% scouts) and delegates to collective-intelligence-coordinator, swarm-memory-manager, worker-specialist, scout-explorer. Coordination happens through the swarm/ memory namespace, not direct calls.

Agents are markdown files with YAML frontmatter (in practice mostly name+description; richer ones exist) under domain dirs: core (coder/tester/reviewer/architect), consensus (byzantine/raft/gossip/quorum/crdt), github, neural, sona, sparc, optimization, testing, specialized/mobile, payments. Each .md is a system prompt with Core Responsibilities + Implementation Approach + Collaboration (named integration points to sibling agents). Frontmatter-only agent persona files = SA-09.

Dedicated consensus-protocol agents implement multi-agent agreement: byzantine-coordinator runs 3-phase PBFT tolerating f<n/3 malicious nodes with cryptographic message validation and partition recovery, collaborating with Security Manager / Quorum Manager / CRDT Synchronizer. Realizes adaptive-consensus/graph-verified debate as first-class agents.

Beyond doers, the hive splits out infrastructure personas: swarm-memory-manager owns state persistence/records (the memory-transfer bridge to fresh agents) and scout-explorer does environmental scanning/threat detection — a separation of coordination, memory, and exploration as distinct agents.

Agent defined as markdown + frontmatter (name, description). GTD decision-tree persona: loads unprocessed daily-note items, classifies (non-actionable useful->reference / not useful->delete / actionable <2min->do now / >2min->task or project), marks processed, reports counts via Telegram HTML. Invoked on heavy inbox, weekly review, or /inbox.

Markdown+frontmatter agent for graph hygiene: 7-step scan -> orphan detection (no incoming links + not in any MOC) -> link suggestions ranked by topic/temporal/goal overlap -> duplicate flagging (title fuzzy + phrase overlap + tag match) -> MOC refresh. Reports connectivity % = (notes with 2+ links / total) x 100.

Bootstrap fan-out: swarm_prepare.py bins the whole vault into ~50K-token batches, dispatches each to a HAIKU model for cheap initial classification (Wave 1); swarm_reduce.py consolidates and sends to a SONNET model for final schema validation (Wave 2). Model-tiered (cheap-triage/frontier-validate), parallel-by-batch, map-reduce shape. Hard rule: 'Always run Phase 2B — script alone cannot classify unstructured content.' Runs with NO API key, driven through the subscription session.

Scheduled jobs run in a 'separate cron-session' with NO access to the live conversation context, so each scheduled prompt must be fully self-contained (paths/criteria/instructions embedded). Distinct from the one long-lived interactive main session — a deliberate one-task-one-clean-context split, both billed under the subscription, with results delivered back to Telegram.

examples/tutorials/subagents.md teaches (not ships) the agent-definition-as-markdown pattern: a subagent is a .md file with YAML frontmatter (name/description/tools) + a system prompt body, placed in .claude/agents/ or .factory/droids/, invoked explicitly via the /agents command. Worked example is a blog-writing agent; another walkthrough has Claude Code read context files and synthesize a PRD-writer agent. Deliberately minimal — 'your first subagent can be 10 lines', sequential single-agent use, no parallel fan-out or orchestration.

Pattern_raw_name: Optional subagent delegation pattern (Research → Planning → Verification)
How_implemented: AGENTS.md defines an OPTIONAL, runtime-permitting subagent chain in fixed order: (1) Research subagent extracts facts/constraints from target files only; (2) Planning subagent produces actionable steps tied to goals + task schema; (3) Verification subagent checks outputs/validations/completion evidence. Main agent defines scope, prevents overlap, and synthesizes one final answer. Explicitly forbidden for simple single-file edits / status updates / one-step requests.

Every subagent response MUST include three fields — assumptions made, result summary, and checks run (or explicitly 'not run'). The main agent consumes only this compact structured report and produces the single final response, so each subagent's raw file-reading output stays out of the main context.

Read-only, single-turn, isolated sub-agent (tools: no write) that stress-tests a PM artifact against its originating skill's SKILL.md contract read at invocation time. Grades findings P0–P3, maps artifact type → standards doc (PRD→deliver-prd+deliver-edge-cases, OKR→foundation-okr-writer, persona→foundation-persona, etc.), checks cross-artifact coherence (does PRD metric match hypothesis measurement plan?), falsifiability, evidence traceability. Every finding must carry a concrete fix ('this is unclear' is invalid). Has a refusal protocol returning a P0 when inputs are incomplete/out-of-scope/placeholder. Fresh-context skeptic that never authors.

frontmatter: tools: Skill,Read,Grep,Glob,Bash,Edit; model: inherit; memory: none; explicit-invocation-only. Two input modes — Mode A parses a foundation-prioritized-action-plan output (extracts Section-7 prompt blocks in order, top-3 executable cap) or Mode B a user-named chain. Per step it classifies the result PRODUCED / EMPTY / FAILED and runs either CHECKPOINTED (pause for approve/edit/skip/redo after each step) or GUARDED-AUTO (--auto: advance on PRODUCED, force pause on EMPTY, halt on FAILED). Steps are ORDERED-INDEPENDENT by default (no artifact threading) unless --thread; persists artifacts to disk per run.

Non-user-facing dispatcher sub-agent that scores an incoming query against every skill description: field and routes to the single best-matching skill — a 'key-free engine' selecting on semantic intent. This is the runtime arm of the trigger-fixtures/router-eval testing.

Cross-cutting repo-governance sub-agent that lints the skill catalog against structural and quality criteria (the structural-compliance role pm-critic explicitly defers to). Complements the CI validators with an agent-driven audit pass.

Orchestrates a 6-gate (G0–G4) pre-tag release runbook, validating gate-to-gate progression and refusing gate bypasses — a sequential gated pipeline agent for release governance. Also owns style-rule enforcement at release time.
Generates CHANGELOG entries from the git log following conventional-commits, referencing only public paths and GitHub issues/PRs (per CLAUDE.md public-vs-private rules).

Markdown+YAML-frontmatter agent (name, description, tools: Read/WebSearch/WebFetch/Bash — a least-privilege, READ-ONLY allowlist; no Write/Edit). Takes one TaskMaster task, runs 3-5 targeted queries (prefers task-master research / MCP over raw WebSearch), returns a 25-40 line cited summary and NOTHING else — the parent does the tasks.json writeback. Up to 5 run concurrently per dependency wave (fan-out). Constraints: no hallucinated sources, flag unresolved questions, graceful tool-fallback.

The execute-fleet orchestrator is a planner/manager that splits the graph into file-disjoint dependency waves, then spawns model-agnostic workers each in its own git worktree+branch with task JSON embedded in-prompt. Workers route by a capability ladder ('Fable for hardest/frontier tier down to haiku for trivial'). They report via durable inbox with 4 fixed terminal statuses; verified branches merge one-at-a-time into fleet-integration behind a checker gate between merges — the canonical dependency-wave fleet pattern. Sole-writer rule keeps the control plane race-free.

Pattern_raw_name: Per-task execution subagent with complexity-tiered model + triple checkers
How_implemented: In execute-task step 7, the implementation subagent is dispatched on a complexity→model tier (1-4 fast/haiku-class, 5-7 standard, 8-10 capable/frontier) so nothing defaults to an expensive model. Step 9 then runs 'three independent checkers' plus a fresh-context reachability sweep and a hard exit-code oracle against the spec — adversarial/fresh-context verification so the builder cannot self-certify.

CLAUDE.md describes (roadmap/north-star) a 'tournament marketplace' where N executors race one job, are adjudicated through Gate 5 + Gate 6, and the winner is paid in AtlasCoin; security uses commit-reveal sha256(diff base..HEAD) to defeat diff-copy attacks, with an independent 'watcher' re-execution and fail-closed slashing. This is an adversarial multi-executor consensus design (described in the constitution, not all shipped).

execute.md: after structure produces a task, an analysis file (.claude/epics/<epic>/<N>-analysis.md) splits one issue into independent work streams ('### Stream A: <Name> / Scope / Files / Can Start: immediately / Dependencies'). The orchestrator launches one Task agent per stream that can start immediately (max 5 concurrent), each scoped to its assigned files only, with dependent streams queued until prerequisites finish. This is planner->worker fan-out with least-privilege file scoping, not role personas.

Pattern_raw_name: git worktree isolation for epic agents
How_implemented: sync.md creates one worktree per epic: `git worktree add ../epic-<name> -b epic/<name>` off a freshly pulled main. All of that epic's parallel agents operate inside this single worktree (file-scope isolation prevents collisions), keeping main stable and giving clean git isolation; coordination is via frequent 'Issue #N' commits, agents check `git status <file>` before touching shared files, and '--force' is forbidden.

depends_on / parallel / conflicts_with frontmatter on task files plus 'Can Start / Dependencies' fields in the analysis streams gate which agents may run: independent streams fan out immediately, blocked ones wait for prerequisites, and conflicts_with marks tasks touching identical files as non-concurrent — a dependency-wave fleet, capped at 5 concurrent.

Both sync.md and structure.md offload bulk work to parallel Task agents: when >5 tasks must become GitHub issues, batches of 3-4 are created concurrently by parallel agents; medium/large epic decomposition similarly batches task-file creation across up to 5 agents. Fan-out is used for throughput on the metadata operations themselves, not just code.

Each persona is a SKILL.md (agent-* folder) + customize.toml defining role/identity/communication_style/principles/icon, plus a deterministic 8-step activation (resolve customize → prepend steps → adopt persona → load persistent facts → resolve config → greet by name w/ icon → append steps → dispatch/menu). Persona persists across skill invocations (icon-prefixed every message) until dismissed. Roster centrally declared in config.yaml with codes/titles/icons.

Persona agents are dispatchers: PM's menu routes to bmad-prd / create-epics-and-stories / check-implementation-readiness / correct-course; analyst, architect, dev each front their phase's skills. The agent is the human-facing orchestrator; the actual work lives in the routed component skills (skill:name invocation only, enforced by the validator).

The numbered phase folders enforce an ordered specialist chain — analysis (brief/PRFAQ) → planning (PRD/UX) → solutioning (architecture/epics/stories) → implementation (dev/QA/review). Each specialist hands a file artifact to the next (CM-08), and the story file specifically transfers compacted working knowledge (Dev Notes + References) to a fresh dev agent context.

Convenes a group discussion among installed BMAD agents/custom personas in ONE session. Roster resolves via 'uv run scripts/resolve_party.py' (default group or all installed; override with --party <id> or inline cast). Default 'session' mode voices every persona inline as '{icon} **{name}:** …' short turns woven by an orchestrator; optional 'auto'/'subagent' modes spawn independent agent reasoning, 'agent-team' mode runs persistent teams. Per-party memoization persists memory across sessions.

Fresh-context skeptic reviewers: adversarial-general adopts a jaded 'expect sloppy work' persona, must surface ≥10 issues (missing elements, logical gaps, unstated assumptions), and HALTS treating zero findings as suspicious — explicitly fresh-context, no prior goodwill, focuses on what's ABSENT. edge-case-hunter is a paired critic specialist. Used to review specs/diffs/docs against confirmation bias.

3+ judge 'agents' score each atomic claim; the pipeline computes a Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic across their score distribution and early-stops when stable (KS<0.1 or variance<0.02), claimed to save 30-50% of LLM calls. Implemented as step 4 of the Unified Verification Pipeline in the Swift IntelligenceEngine package, not as Claude subagent .md files.

When judges diverge (variance>0.1) the pipeline opens debate rounds: round 1 independent evals, later rounds share opinions and request reassessment, stopping at variance<0.05 or max 3 rounds. Conditionally routed by Complexity-Aware Selection only for CRITICAL claims (≥0.75).

A deterministic, no-LLM verifier that builds a claim-relationship graph and detects cycles, contradictions, orphaned requirements and ranks importance via PageRank BEFORE any judge is invoked — a free fresh-context structural critic that filters problems and feeds complexity scoring. Pipeline step 3.

Pipeline step 1 scores each claim's complexity and routes it: SIMPLE skips graph analysis, MODERATE adds graph checks, COMPLEX activates NLI entailment, CRITICAL triggers multi-agent debate — a dispatcher that allocates progressively more expensive verification (and which of the 15 strategies) to harder claims, i.e. effort/model-tiering by claim.

A pub/sub bus lets the reasoning enhancements (Buffer, Expansion, Metacognitive, Collaborative, Stall-Recovery) emit and subscribe to stall/convergence/confidence-drop signals in real time, giving bidirectional feedback loops between the engines — a shared-memory swarm coordination layer rather than isolated agents.

Markdown+frontmatter agent (name/description/model:opus/color:green). Does NOT execute work; uses MCP tools get_tasks/get_task to build a dependency graph, then for each independent task/group deploys a task-executor with a minimal-but-sufficient context packet (task id, objective, dependency info, success criteria, project context, reporting expectations). Maximizes parallelization by picking unblocked tasks and never assigning dependent tasks concurrently; on each completion it reassesses the graph for newly-unblocked work. Runs on the frontier model (opus) while delegating execution to cheaper models — explicit model tiering.

model:sonnet/color:blue 'implementation specialist'. Pulls full task via `task-master show <id>`, checks dependency conflicts, implements per project standards, runs the task's testStrategy, logs decisions via update-subtask, flips status in-progress→done, and returns a summary + recommended next step to the orchestrator. Operates on a fresh delegated context fed only the orchestrator's packet plus the task object it fetches itself.

model:sonnet/color:yellow QA gate for tasks in 'review' status. READ-ONLY tools only (Read/Grep/Bash) — explicitly cannot fix, only verify. Adversarially checks 5 dimensions (spec compliance, implementation accuracy, test success via real compile/test runs, code quality, dependency integrity), separates critical failures from minor issues, and emits a PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL verdict; failures bounce back to the executor for re-implementation. Least-privilege tool scoping enforces the no-self-grading firewall.

Pattern_raw_name: context: fork subagent capability (FORK_CONTEXT_COMMANDS) — present but disabled
How_implemented: claude/__init__.py contains the machinery to run a command in a forked subagent context (per Claude Code's run-skills-in-a-subagent), keyed per-command. It is intentionally EMPTY: /analyze was forked (#2511) on the assumption its heavy reads collapse to a short summary, but in practice it returns a 300-500 line report injected back into the main conversation, so each later fork inherited the growing context until the chat froze (#3185). The maintainers' rule: no command opts into context:fork until it genuinely returns a compact result — a deliberate, documented rejection of a leaky context-firewall.

Fixed ordered chain orchestrated by the /prd-new command (model opus): product-interrogator -> official-researcher -> research-validator -> requirements-analyst -> architecture-designer -> prd-writer -> stack-guide-generator -> skills-generator -> cicd-generator. Each is a markdown+frontmatter agent (name/description/tools/model/color) under agents/. Distinct named role personas (analyst, researcher, auditor, architect, writer, generators), color-coded (blue/green/red/purple).

color:red sonnet agent (tools WebFetch/WebSearch) invoked in a fresh Agent context with ONLY the research_refs — explicitly to prevent confirmation bias ('cannot rationalize accepting bad research because it fits what we decided'). Runs a 5-point checklist (officiality, URL reachability, recency/version, factual consistency, completeness), returns approved/rejected per ref with requery_guidance; orchestrator loops re-research up to 3x then marks partially_validated.

prd-new dispatches one official-researcher per pending_research item as multiple Agent calls in a single message (parallel). Each is tool-scoped to WebSearch/WebFetch only and hard-constrained by a long blocked_domains list (reddit/medium/stackoverflow/blogs/social/wikipedia) so only vendor docs, standards bodies, and gov portals are consulted.

Pattern_raw_name: Model-tiering and least-privilege tool scoping per agent
How_implemented: architecture-designer runs on opus (the reasoning-heavy stack-design step); all other 8 agents run on sonnet. Tools are scoped per role: product-interrogator and requirements-analyst get Read only; researchers get WebSearch/WebFetch; the generators (skills/stack-guide/cicd) get Write/Edit/Read/Bash. Orchestrating commands run on opus.

After architecture approval, prd-new dispatches prd-writer + stack-guide-generator + skills-generator + cicd-generator as four Agent calls in one message (parallel), then runs project-docs CREATE mode sequentially, then a verification step listing all 23 expected files and regenerating any missing one.

Pattern_raw_name: Planner → Manager → Workers (Spec/Plan/Rules then dispatched Tasks)
How_implemented: Three named personas in SEPARATE contexts. Planner does discovery and emits Spec+Plan+Rules + populates `.apm/`. Manager reads the Plan and produces self-contained Task Prompts, reviews Reports, maintains state via execution summaries (not raw code). Workers are domain-scoped (frontend/backend/API): each registers to its bus slug, accepts ONLY tasks for its identifier, executes→validates→logs→reports, and explicitly avoids reasoning about broader project state. Classic planner→workers delegation with filesystem handoff.

A markdown+frontmatter agent (name/description) in `templates/agents/`. The Planner spawns it during context-gathering; it reads `.apm/archives/` summaries-first, synthesizes into five fixed categories (scope, design decisions, completed work, known issues, verification handles), and returns ONLY that compact synthesis plus file-path spot-check handles — its raw archive traversal never enters the Planner's context (context firewall), and the verification handles let the Planner adversarially check for stale context.

Inside the task-execution loop, if validation fails after the Worker's own investigation, the Worker spawns a debug subagent with structured error details and instructions to trace root causes 'particularly when multiple independent areas could be responsible' — i.e. fresh-context root-cause tracing, optionally fanned out across independent suspect areas, returning findings to the Worker.

Pattern_raw_name: Per-agent Handoff = context transfer to fresh instance
How_implemented: Both Manager (apm-6) and Worker (apm-7) have handoff commands. Instance numbers are tracked in the handoff log frontmatter; an incoming instance reads the Handoff Log + Handoff Prompt off its bus, clears the bus, reconstructs from logs, and confirms readiness — preserving domain familiarity across a model's context-window boundary as a deliberate one-task-many-sessions continuity mechanism.

Pattern_raw_name: Version-control isolation in tracker (branches/worktrees per task)
How_implemented: tracker.md carries a per-task Branch column and a Version Control table (base branch, branch convention, commit convention); the Manager handoff log tracks 'active branches, worktrees, pending merges' — workers operate on isolated branches/worktrees to avoid collisions during parallel task execution.

Pattern_raw_name: Sub-Agent Roles table (Engineer / Designer / Executive / Skeptic / Customer / Data Analyst)
How_implemented: NOT Claude Code subagents — there is no .claude/agents/ directory (confirmed 404). Instead a 6-row markdown table inside CLAUDE.md defines persona Role | Lens | Key Questions (Engineer/Feasibility, Designer/Usability, Executive/Strategy, Skeptic/Risk, Customer/Value, Data Analyst/Measurement). Activation is a verbal contract: 'When I say review as [role], fully adopt that perspective.' README prescribes SEQUENTIAL chaining within a single session ('Write a PRD -> Review as engineer -> fix gaps -> Review as skeptic -> tighten -> Review as customer -> simplify. Three passes, three minutes'). So this is a lightweight reviewer/critic persona chain implemented as in-context role-swaps, not spawned isolated agents — meaning no real context firewall (CM-06 deliberately NOT present) and no per-agent tool scoping beyond the 'lens' framing. The Skeptic role ('what could go wrong? untested assumptions?') functions as the adversarial-review persona.

Pattern_raw_name: Agent .md file (markdown + YAML frontmatter system-prompt anatomy)
How_implemented: Every agent is ONE markdown file. Frontmatter fields: name (kebab-case, must match invocation), description (a usage sentence FOLLOWED BY 3-4 inline <example>…</example> blocks, each containing Context / user / assistant / <commentary> sub-tags — these examples ARE the auto-invocation training signal, not just docs), color (UI tint), tools (explicit least-privilege allowlist, e.g. sprint-prioritizer gets 'Write, Read, TodoWrite, Grep'; trend-researcher gets 'WebSearch, WebFetch, Read, Write, Grep'; studio-coach uniquely gets 'Task'). Body is a 500+ word system prompt with a RIGID repeated anatomy: 2nd-person identity paragraph → numbered '**Your primary responsibilities**' (each a bolded category with 6 sub-bullets) → named **framework blocks** → **Anti-Patterns** → **Templates** (fenced code) → **Metrics**. Tool scoping per agent is the least-privilege mechanism (no agent gets everything).

Product-division persona for 6-day sprint planning/prioritization. Encodes named PM frameworks as a literal bulleted menu under '**Prioritization Frameworks**': RICE (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort), Value-vs-Effort matrices, Kano model, Jobs-to-be-Done, user story mapping, OKR alignment. Hard-codes a '**6-Week Sprint Structure**' week-by-week timeline (W1 planning/quick-wins → W2-3 core → W4 integration/test → W5 polish → W6 launch). Ships a fenced **Decision Template** (Feature/User Problem/Success Metric/Effort/Risk/Priority[P0-P2]/Decision[Include-Defer-Cut]) as an enforced output contract, plus an explicit '**Sprint Anti-Patterns**' list (over-committing, perfectionism over shipping, mid-sprint pivots). Tools restricted to Write/Read/TodoWrite/Grep.

Product persona that aggregates multi-source user feedback into prioritized insight. Sections: Multi-Source Aggregation, Pattern Recognition/Theme Extraction, Sentiment & Urgency Scoring (4-tier Critical→Low urgency matrix), Actionable Insight Generation, Feedback-Loop Optimization. Encodes feedback CATEGORIES (Bug/Feature/UX-friction/Performance/Content/Monetization/Onboarding) and named complaint archetypes ('Love it but…', 'Almost perfect except…'). Enforced output: a 'Feedback Summary' template (Top-3 Issues w/ fix, Top-3 Feature Requests w/ effort+impact, Quick Wins, week-over-week Sentiment Trends) plus a SMART insight-quality checklist. Tools: Read/Write/Grep/WebFetch/MultiEdit.

Market-trend analyst persona tuned to translate viral social signals into 6-day-buildable products. Distinctive mechanism: encodes QUANTITATIVE decision thresholds directly in prose — a '**Decision Framework**' that gates on trend momentum (<1wk = too early; 1-4wk = perfect for a 6-day sprint; >8wk = saturated), '**Key Metrics**' with numeric triggers (>50% week-over-week hashtag growth = high potential; min 100K potential users), a 5-point '**Trend Evaluation Criteria**', and a '**Red Flags to Avoid**' list (single-influencer fragility, platform-dependent features, legally questionable mechanics). Tools: WebSearch/WebFetch/Read/Write/Grep.

Project-management persona for cross-team coordination/resource allocation. Frontmatter description begins 'PROACTIVELY use this agent…' and states it 'Should be triggered automatically when team dependencies arise' — the proactive-trigger convention. Encodes org-design vocabulary as named blocks: '**Team Topology Patterns**' (Feature/Platform/Tiger/Innovation-Pod/Support-Rotation teams), '**Resource Allocation Frameworks**' (70-20-10 rule, Skill Matrix, Surge Protocols), '**Workflow Optimization Techniques**' (Value Stream Mapping, Theory-of-Constraints, WIP limits), and a fenced 'Team Sync Template'. Its own '6-Week Cycle Management' timeline differs from sprint-prioritizer's (W0 pre-sprint → W6 retro). Tools: Read/Write/MultiEdit/Grep/Glob/TodoWrite.

The only agent granted the 'Task' tool, making it the dispatcher: its system prompt's '**Strategic Orchestration**' section coordinates multi-agent efforts — clarifying each agent's role, preventing duplicate work, creating handoffs between specialists. Frontmatter is 'PROACTIVELY use… when complex multi-agent tasks begin, when agents seem stuck.' Notably the <example> blocks include an agent-to-coach turn ('agent: "I'm having trouble identifying the right trends…"'), modeling agent→orchestrator escalation. Orchestration is prompt-and-Task-tool only — there is NO router config, plan file, or graph; coordination is emergent from Claude Code's native subagent dispatch matching the description field.

Agents grouped by studio department as plain directories: engineering/(7), design/(5), marketing/(7), product/(3: feedback-synthesizer, sprint-prioritizer, trend-researcher), project-management/(3: studio-producer, project-shipper, experiment-tracker), studio-operations/(5), testing/(5), bonus/(2: studio-coach, joker). The folder is purely organizational — install flattens everything into ~/.claude/agents/, so directory has no runtime role (no routing, no scoping by folder).

Agent defined as .cursor/agents/discovery-researcher.md with frontmatter (name, description as auto-invoke trigger, model: inherit). Orchestrates a fixed 5-step sequential discovery pipeline by CALLING SKILLS as sub-steps: /create-interview-snapshots → /synthesize-snapshots → /create-opportunities (with /analyze-metrics for quantitative backing) → /generate-solutions (gate: min 3, ideally 10–15 user ideas, will NOT proceed until user submits ideas) → /identify-test-assumptions. Premise-validation guard runs /product-diagnostic first if the request is solution-driven not problem-driven. Per-step checkpoint menu (Proceed / Revise / Go Deeper / Go Back) and error recovery (resume from any step using saved artifacts). On completion surfaces all artifacts and hands off to initiative-planner, carrying forward top opportunities + critical assumptions.

Planner→workers orchestrator (.cursor/agents/initiative-planner.md, model: inherit). 8-phase delivery pipeline with conditional branching: Phase 0 reads existing discovery artifacts from the initiative dir → Phase 1 /setup-initiative → /create-one-pager → /create-prd (gate: requires explicit one-pager approval before proceeding) → /create-design-brief → /generate-figma-prompt → /build-prototype → /generate-tasks → optional /calculate-ice-score. Artifact handoff is file-based: each phase consumes the approved markdown of the prior phase (1-pager→PRD→design/tasks). Resumable from any phase via saved artifacts. Continues the document-as-baton chain begun by discovery-researcher.

CPO-persona reviewer agent that runs three review frameworks and synthesizes: PRISM strategy review across 7 dimensions (scores positioning, options, scenarios, stakeholder perspectives), Vision review on 4 criteria (inspiring, feasible, constraint-free, grounded in a user problem), and OKR quality/alignment review. Operates as a 'direct, evidence-based sparring partner' — challenges assumptions and offers concrete alternatives rather than surface critique (adversarial/critic stance). Gap-handling: when source docs are missing it offers to draft them OR flags specific gaps before scoring, refusing speculative assessment (anti-hallucination). Wraps the product-strategy-review / product-vision-review / okr-sparring-partner skills.

Pattern_raw_name: Agent definition = single markdown file with YAML frontmatter
How_implemented: Each of the 232 agents is one .md file under a division folder. Frontmatter fields: name, description (the auto-invocation trigger / routing text), color, emoji, vibe (a one-line persona hook), and optional tools (e.g. PM = 'WebFetch, WebSearch, Read, Write, Edit'—a least-privilege allowlist). Body follows a fixed anatomy: Identity&Memory → Core Mission → Critical Rules → Technical Deliverables → Workflow Process → Communication Style → Success Metrics → Personality Highlights. Named role personas (PM is 'Alex, 10+yr PM'; Senior PM is 'SeniorProjectManager').

examples/nexus-spatial-discovery.md documents a fan-out: 8 specialist agents (Product Trend Researcher, Backend Architect, Brand Guardian, Growth Hacker, UX Researcher, Support Responder, XR Interface Architect, Project Shepherd) each got the SAME parallel prompt template ('You are the [ROLE]. Given this opportunity… Work independently. Do NOT coordinate with other agents. Focus on depth in your domain.') and ran for ~10 min with zero cross-talk. A final 'Section 10' synthesis pass surfaces where they independently CONVERGED (4 signals, e.g. '2D-first, spatial-second') and lists 4 unresolved TENSIONS (e.g. $29 vs $99 pricing) flagged for human decision—deliberate non-consensus to avoid papering over real tradeoffs.

Pattern_raw_name: Division roster as a specialist team preset
How_implemented: 232 agents are organized into 16 divisions (Engineering 33, Marketing 32, Specialized 54, Product 5, Project Management 7, etc.). The Product division = Manager, Feedback Synthesizer, Sprint Prioritizer, Trend Researcher, Behavioral Nudge Engine; Project Management = Senior PM, Project Shepherd, Studio Producer, Studio Operations, Experiment Tracker, Jira Workflow Steward, Meeting Notes Specialist. Divisions act as named panels you can install/invoke as a set; the Specialized division explicitly includes 'multi-agent orchestration' agents.

examples/ also holds sequential multi-agent pipelines (workflow-startup-mvp, workflow-landing-page, workflow-book-chapter) where one agent's output feeds the next; workflow-with-memory.md upgrades that manual handoff chain into autonomous tag-addressed memory recall between stages (e.g. Sprint Prioritizer → Backend Architect → Frontend Developer → Reality Checker, the last recalling ALL project-tagged work for a review pass).

Pattern_raw_name: agents.yaml role personas (product_manager, product_owner, designer, subject_matter_expert, quality_assurance_designer)
How_implemented: Five agents are defined declaratively in agents.yaml with role/goal/backstory fields, each interpolating the runtime {topic}. crew.py binds each YAML block to a crewAI @agent-decorated method (config=self.agents_config['<name>'], verbose=True) — framework-defined agents, no per-agent tools or model overrides. The PM owns vision + final synthesis; PO owns backlog + acceptance criteria; SME provides domain critique; Designer owns UX/solution design; QA Designer owns the quality plan.

crew.py builds the Crew with process=Process.sequential and tasks auto-collected from @task decorators, so the 7 tasks run in fixed file order: purpose(PM) -> features(PM) -> SME review -> design(Designer) -> backlog(PO) -> QA plan(QA) -> PRD synthesis(PM). The hierarchical option is present but commented out. The SME task is a review/critique stage feeding the designer — an embedded reviewer-in-the-line.

The SME task description instructs the agent to 'conduct a thorough review of the features list ... provide feedback on how each feature helps address user needs or how they do not' and to surface aspects the PM overlooked; its output is fed as context into design_task. A sequential reviewer/critic persona embedded mid-chain rather than a separate adversarial pass.

Markdown+frontmatter agent (model: sonnet, tools Read/Glob/Grep/Task). Body holds an explicit routing TABLE (Market->market-analyst, Users->research-ops, Vision->product-strategist, Roadmap->roadmap-builder, Prioritization->feature-prioritizer, Specs->requirements-engineer, Launch->launch-planner), a 4-step routing procedure (analyze -> gather product-context -> Task() the specialist with full context injected -> handle multi-step by sequencing), a 6-stage product journey map, and a per-specialist skill-loadout list. 'One specialist at a time' — routes sequentially, passes context between them (doc/payload handoff), not parallel fan-out.

Pattern_raw_name: 7 PM specialist agents (market-analyst, research-ops, product-strategist, roadmap-builder, feature-prioritizer, requirements-engineer, launch-planner)
How_implemented: Each is a .md persona with frontmatter declaring model, memory scope, a least-privilege `tools:` allowlist (doc-writers add Write + permissionMode: acceptEdits; analysts stay read-only), and a `skills:` list binding that agent to its framework skills (e.g. requirements-engineer -> specification-techniques/prd-templates/user-story-templates). Tools are scoped per role.

Deliberately model: haiku ('utility agent... uses Haiku for efficient file operations') while PM specialists run sonnet/opus — explicit model-tiering: cheap model for file-scan grunt work, frontier models for reasoning. memory: local, permissionMode: acceptEdits, tools Read/Glob/Grep/Write, skills: codebase-scanning.

The /agent-teams:validation-sprint command spawns 3 teammates IN PARALLEL via Claude Code Agent Teams (Phase 2), each with a distinct lens; Phase 3 runs ONE round of mutual cross-examination (each agent gets the other two's reports and challenges them); Phase 4 lead does weighted synthesis into a BUILD/DON'T BUILD/NEEDS-MORE-EVIDENCE verdict with dissent documented. idea-skeptic is model: opus, a dedicated adversarial/devil's-advocate verifier whose explicit job is 'try to kill the idea' using a named attack arsenal (vitamin-not-painkiller, feature-not-product, temporary-differentiation, TAM-fantasy) and a 10-question arsenal, rating each attack FATAL/SERIOUS/CONCERNING and disclosing its own confidence.

Two more bundled panels: prd-stress-test runs 3 reviewers (market-fit-reviewer, feasibility-reviewer, scope-reviewer) scoring a PRD independently then flagging conflicts -> READY/NEEDS-REVISION/MAJOR-REWORK; competitive-war-room runs one researcher per named competitor in parallel, synthesized into a positioning map + battle cards. Same independent-investigate -> cross-reference -> synthesize protocol from team-coordination.

Markdown+frontmatter agent (name, description, tools: [Read,Write,Edit,Bash,Glob,Grep], model: opus). System prompt encodes named PM methodologies: JTBD personas, 'As a [persona], I want [capability], so that [outcome]' stories, Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, RICE prioritization, MVP scoping. Enforced output contract: PRD with problem statement + quantified impact, success metrics with baselines, ≥3 acceptance criteria per story, in/out scope, rollout + rollback criteria, versioned PRD with changelog. Standalone — no handoff/orchestration language in the prompt.

Agent persona (tools same six, model opus) as a 'process guardian'. Body is a 10-step operational playbook (sprint planning → standups → impediment tracking → burndown → review → retro → backlog coaching → velocity trending → anti-pattern detection → scope protection) plus quantified verification thresholds (timeboxes met, 48h impediment resolution, ±20% velocity variance). Escalates >48h impediments to management; otherwise standalone, no multi-agent collaboration.

Coordinator persona that enumerates named WORKFLOW patterns (not named agents): Pipeline (sequential transform), Map-Reduce (parallel fan-out → synthesis agent fan-in), Supervisor (planner decomposes → assigns specialists → reviews), Debate (two opposing agents + a judge), Iterative Refinement (draft/review cycles). Routes by DAG topological sort for dependencies; matches work to agents by specialization/availability/historical performance. Roles are functional (planner/specialist/reviewer/synthesis/judge), dynamically assigned, not hardwired endpoints.

Pattern_raw_name: agents/ catalog — 135 agents in 10 category folders, invoked by CLAUDE.md reference
How_implemented: All agents are .md files with identical frontmatter shape (name/description/tools-allowlist/model). Categories: core-development, language-experts, infrastructure, quality-assurance, data-ai, developer-experience, specialized-domains, business-product, orchestration, research-analysis. Invocation pattern per README is by-reference: you cite 'agents/<cat>/<name>.md' in your project CLAUDE.md rather than calling a Task subagent_type — so it's a personas-as-files library you compose manually. tools arrays act as a least-privilege allowlist per agent.

Each is a single .md file = YAML frontmatter (name, description as 'Use this agent when…' trigger, tools as a comma-list allowlist [Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, WebSearch — least-privilege, no Bash for these doc-roles], model routed per-role: haiku for product/project/scrum, sonnet for business-analyst) followed by a system prompt with an identical rigid anatomy across all roles: (1) one-line senior-role identity; (2) a numbered 'When invoked' invocation protocol (3-4 steps: query context-manager → review materials → analyze → implement); (3) ~8-12 domain checklists each containing exactly 8 items (e.g. PM: strategy domains, roadmap framework, prioritization methods, product frameworks, lifecycle phases, analytics, stakeholder mgmt, launch checklist); (4) a 'Communication Protocol' section with the JSON context-request block; (5) a 3-phase Development Workflow (Discovery/Planning → Implementation → <Domain> Excellence); (6) an 8-item 'integrate with' collaborator list naming sibling agents. Frontmatter+body IS the whole agent — no separate config/tools file.

An 'agent-installer' subagent lets Claude Code itself browse this GitHub repo and install agents on request ('find PHP agents and install php-pro globally'). Separately, every agent closes with an 8-item integration list naming sibling agents (PM → ux-researcher, engineering, business-analyst, marketing, sales-engineer, customer-success, data-analyst, scrum-master), forming an implicit hand-off/router graph across the 154-agent catalog — but there is no orchestrator agent that enforces routing; it's advisory text inside each persona.

Each agent is plugins/<name>/agents/<n>.md with frontmatter: name, description (carries 'Use PROACTIVELY for...' auto-trigger), model (opus|sonnet|haiku|inherit, alias-mapped per harness at generation time), optional tools allowlist, color. e.g. business-analyst uses model: sonnet with no tools; team-lead uses model: opus with a 12-tool allowlist (Read/Glob/Grep/Bash/Agent/TeamCreate/Task*/SendMessage). Model-tiering and least-privilege tool-scoping are per-agent frontmatter facts.

team-lead (opus) decomposes work into parallelizable units with explicit acceptance criteria + complexity estimates, assigns EXCLUSIVE file ownership per teammate to avoid write conflicts, builds a dependency graph to sequence critical path while maximizing parallelism, then synthesizes outputs (resolving conflicts via tiebreak criteria) into one consolidated report. Workers report at milestones, not continuously. Carries a stated bias toward smaller teams with clearer ownership.

Worker agent scoped to Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep/Bash/Task*/SendMessage with hard rules 'only modify files assigned to you / never touch shared files'; on unclear requirements it messages the lead rather than deciding autonomously; returns a clean summary (changes + integration concerns + deviations) — treating unassigned improvements as observations, not work. Enforces context-firewall discipline so raw worker churn doesn't leak into the orchestrator's context.

/team-spawn <preset> provisions named team panels: e.g. 'security' = 4 reviewers on distinct domains (OWASP/auth/dependencies/secrets); 'debug' = competing-hypothesis investigators collecting evidence in parallel; 'migration' = lead planner + parallel implementers + correctness verifier. Preset defines member count + role distribution; spawned via TeamCreate then per-member Agent calls with role-specific prompts; coordinated via /team-delegate and monitored via /team-status.

Pattern_raw_name: feature-dev code-explorer / code-architect agents (.md + frontmatter, tool-scoped, model-pinned)
How_implemented: agents/code-explorer.md is a markdown agent definition: frontmatter name + description (the routing trigger) + an explicit tools allowlist (Glob,Grep,LS,Read,NotebookRead,WebFetch,TodoWrite,WebSearch,KillShell,BashOutput — read/analysis only, no Edit/Write) + model:sonnet + color, then a system-prompt body specifying a tracing methodology and a required output contract (entry points with file:line, execution flow, and 'list of files absolutely essential to read'). The /feature-dev command launches 2-3 of these in PARALLEL each targeting a different codebase aspect, then reads the files they return — a fan-out whose context firewall is that agents return file lists/summaries, not raw dumps. code-architect is spawned the same way with minimal/clean/pragmatic foci.

Pattern_raw_name: code-review parallel multi-agent panel with confidence scoring
How_implemented: The /code-review command spawns agents INLINE (no separate agent files) with deliberate model-tiering: cheap Haiku agents for eligibility triage, CLAUDE.md path gathering, and PR summary; then 5 parallel Sonnet reviewers each with a distinct lens (CLAUDE.md adherence / shallow bug scan / git-blame historical context / prior-PR comments / code-comment guidance); then a Haiku agent PER FLAGGED ISSUE that scores 0-100 confidence against a verbatim rubric; issues under 80 are filtered out; a final Haiku re-runs the eligibility check before posting. A reviewer-panel + adversarial-scoring + model-tiered pipeline expressed entirely in one command file.

A markdown agent-definition file with a Role/Inputs/Process/Output-Format/Guidelines contract. Reads a run transcript + outputs_dir, verdicts each assertion PASS/FAIL with cited evidence, and — crucially — adversarially refuses surface compliance ('correct filename but empty content = FAIL', 'burden of proof to pass is on the expectation'). Second job: critique the evals themselves (flag non-discriminating assertions, uncovered outcomes). Emits a strict grading.json (fields text/passed/evidence — the viewer depends on exact names).

agents/comparator.md does a BLIND comparison — two outputs handed to an independent agent with no label of which skill produced which, to kill confirmation bias. agents/analyzer.md then 'unblinds': reads both skills + both transcripts, scores instruction-following 1-10, attributes the win to specific causes, and emits prioritized improvement_suggestions JSON. analyzer.md has a second mode ('Analyzing Benchmark Results') that surfaces cross-run anomalies (non-discriminating assertions, flaky high-variance evals, time/token tradeoffs) as freeform-but-grounded notes.

skill-creator orchestrates: for each test case spawn TWO executor subagents in the SAME turn — one given the skill path, one baseline (no skill / old-snapshot) — launching everything at once so they finish together, each writing to its own outputs/ dir. The main skill is the planner; executors are isolated workers; grader/analyzer are the reviewer chain. A/B paired fan-out is the core measurement primitive.

Subagent prompts live as plain markdown under the skill's own agents/ folder (skill-creator/agents/{grader,comparator,analyzer}.md) and are loaded on demand — SKILL.md says 'Read them when you need to spawn the relevant subagent.' No separate .claude/agents registry; agents are just JIT-loaded reference files the skill points at. Note: these are NOT YAML-frontmatter Claude Code subagents — they are role-prompt docs the orchestrator pastes into a spawned task.
